pi_items.source: OpenICE Supervisor Demo Applications Manual confirms Infusion Safety is 'a prototype implementation of a closed loop safety system' that 'controls the administration of IV medication' and is 'programmed to stop the pump if the algorithm believes the patient is in a non-normal state'. Web search confirms closed-loop drug infusion systems using physiological monitoring (like SpO2) for automated medication control are real and represent an important safety innovation in ICUs.

privileged_info_summary: OpenICE (Open-source Integrated Clinical Environment) is a real medical device interoperability platform developed by the MD PnP Program at Massachusetts General Hospital. The Infusion Safety clinical application is a real component of OpenICE that implements a prototype closed-loop safety system for controlling IV medication infusion. The system stops medication infusion based on patient physiological status when configured safety algorithms are triggered. SpO2 (oxygen saturation) monitoring is a well-established vital sign measurement in ICUs with optimal target ranges typically 90-96%. Closed-loop medication delivery systems that respond to physiological monitoring parameters represent an emerging safety technology in modern critical care. All metadata claims (device roles, application name, file requirements, clinical context, and scoring threshold) are consistent with the actual OpenICE platform and documented clinical safety principles.
